events.event_name: Open Water Course  (Instructor Flemming) [CANCELLED]
events.title: Open Water Course  (Instructor Flemming) [CANCELLED]
events.description:
Are you ready to take the plunge and explore the incredible world beneath the waves? Join our Open Water Diver Course and unlock the gateway to breathtaking underwater adventures.

What You'll Learn:
✅ Essential diving skills
✅ Safety procedures
✅ Equipment use and maintenance
✅ Experience open water dives

Who Can Join?
This course is perfect for beginners and anyone looking to start their scuba diving journey. No prior experience required – just a passion for adventure!

You must be able to swim before starting the course.

What’s Included:
🌟 Professional instruction
🌟 Course materials
🌟 Certification upon completion

Sign Up Today!
Spaces are limited, so don’t miss your chance to explore the underwater world.

To register:
1. Read our training policy at: muuc.org.au/courses/training-policy
2. Create an account on Teamapp including your first and last name 
3. Purchase ticket in this event to secure your spot
4. RSVP yes to the event
5. Make sure to track notifications on this event for official course comms.

Prior to the course:
1. Make sure to go to the teamapp store and do the Liability Waiver, Gear Deposit and purchase the applicable membership category to be eligible to receive the e-learning that must be completed prior to starting the practical component of the course. Please complete ASAP

2. Complete your e-learning after receiving the code from your instructor, the e-learning must be completed at least 1 week prior to the course starting. 

3. Make sure to complete your course orientation as per the request of your instructor

Course inquiries:
Use comments section here or email us:
MUUC Course Coordinator 
[redacted email]

events.event_name: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
events.title: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
events.description:
The course to unlock the best of Victorian Boat Diving! Our boat dives are deep and bottom times are often all too short! With Nitrox diving you can tailor your gas mix to extend your bottom times, and enhance your safety profiles. As the club is equipped with a nitrox compressor, it's never been easier to utilise a nitrox certification!

SDI Computer Nitrox
Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

To attend this course, please purchase a ticketand contact Minh via email to organise for your e-learning to be sent out and plan a suitable date for your practical signoff.
email: [redacted email]

events.event_name: SDI Advanced Adventure Diver Course (30M)  w/ Flemming
events.title: SDI Advanced Adventure Diver Course (30M)  w/ Flemming
events.description:
Course Fee: A$580 (2 boat dives included!)
To lock in your spot please grab a ticket and send Flemming a WhatsApp message on [redacted phone]

SDI Advanced Adventure Diver certifies you to dive up to 30 meters, opening up a wide range of boat diving in Victoria! 

If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge and skills, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education. The remaining specialty dives we will do are:

Advanced Buoyancy Control
Night Diving
Wreck Diving

You will also have the option of doing your Nitrox certification as an add-on to the course for 145$, please contact Flemming if this is something you are interested in.

The course fee includes 2x charterboat dives but does not include transport and gear rental for the 5 dives.

Students must have full scuba gear, including diving computer, SMB, compass and two dive lights.

If suitable club gear is not available, I can help you access special pricing on rentals/purchases of dive gear through local dive stores.

There will be an opt-in option to stay at an Airbnb nearer the dive sites during the course.

Any questions, please feel free to contact Flemming at 
[redacted email] or WhatsApp [redacted phone]

events.event_name: 🌊 [Thursday 19th Feb update] Dive Trip Organiser Workshop 🌊
events.title: 🌊 [Thursday 19th Feb update] Dive Trip Organiser Workshop 🌊
events.description:
Join us for our Dive Trip Organiser Workshop and discover everything you need to know about becoming a future trip organiser for our club! Whether you're a seasoned diver ready to step into a leadership role or simply curious about what it takes to guide a dive trip, this workshop is designed for all club members eager to learn and grow.

I will be hosting this workshop on the 19th of Feb at 5:30pm at social space B (at the back of Nona Lee sports centre, near the running track) -- if you can't find it, head to the shed and shoot us a message, someone will come grab you! 

What You'll Learn:
Pre-Dive Prep: How to check weather conditions, organise gear, and ensure everyone is ready for an epic dive day.
Dive Leadership: Learn how to brief divers, manage groups, and prioritise safety.
Emergency Protocols: What to do in emergency situations and who to contact.
Post-Dive Wrap-Up: Shed cleanup, gear maintenance and keeping things tidy for the next adventure.

Why Join?
Skill Development: Hone your organisational and leadership skills.
Rewarding Experience: Organise trips, make new friends and help others explore the underwater world.

💡 Fun Fact: Trip Organisers are volunteers, not professionals—so the focus is on having fun and creating memorable dives for everyone!

Don't miss this chance to become an essential part of our diving community. Click below to RSVP or ask any questions!

Xiang

events.event_name: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
events.title: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
events.description:
The course to unlock the best of Victorian Boat Diving! Our boat dives are deep and bottom times are often all too short! With Nitrox diving you can tailor your gas mix to extend your bottom times, and enhance your safety profiles. As the club is equipped with a nitrox compressor, it's never been easier to utilise a nitrox certification!

SDI Computer Nitrox
Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

To attend this course, please purchase a ticketand contact Minh via email to organise for your e-learning to be sent out and plan a suitable date for your practical signoff.

events.event_name: Underwater Photography Workshop with Toni
events.title: Underwater Photography Workshop with Toni
events.description:
Interested in improving your underwater photography? Whether you’re new to shooting underwater or looking to refine your technique, this workshop will walk you through the fundamentals of creating compelling underwater images.

Join us for an evening session where we’ll cover the essential elements of underwater photography, including how to set up your camera equipment, understanding the exposure square (ISO, aperture, shutter speed, and strobe power), and how lighting and strobe positioning affect your images underwater. We’ll also explore composition techniques and discuss how to move beyond technical shots to create images that tell a story.

Topics will include:
	•	Underwater camera and housing setup
	•	Understanding the exposure square
	•	Strobe position 
	•	Composition techniques underwater
	•	Turning photos into visual stories

The session will also include example photos and practical tips drawn from real diving situations, competitions, and field experience.

Whether you dive with a compact camera, mirrorless system, or DSLR, you’ll walk away with practical techniques you can apply on your next dive.

📍 Location: Social A
🕢 Time: 7:30 PM – 9:30 PM
📅 Date: March 19

Come curious, bring your questions, and let’s talk about how to capture the underwater world more effectively.

events.event_name: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
events.title: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
events.description:
The course to unlock the best of Victorian Boat Diving! Our boat dives are deep and bottom times are often all too short! With Nitrox diving you can tailor your gas mix to extend your bottom times, and enhance your safety profiles. As the club is equipped with a nitrox compressor, it's never been easier to utilise a nitrox certification!

SDI Computer Nitrox
Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

To attend this course, please purchase a ticketand contact Minh via email to organise for your e-learning to be sent out and plan a suitable date for your practical signoff.
email: [redacted email]

events.event_name: [postponed] SDI Advanced Adventure Diver Course (30M)  w/ Flemming
events.title: [postponed] SDI Advanced Adventure Diver Course (30M)  w/ Flemming
events.description:
Course Fee: A$580 (2 boat dives included!)
To lock in your spot please grab a ticket to lock in your spot and send Flemming a WhatsApp message on [redacted phone]

SDI Advanced Adventure Diver certifies you to dive up to 30 meters, opening up a wide range of boat diving in Victoria! 

If you’ve taken your Open Water Scuba Diver course and are now ready to expand your knowledge and skills, then the SDI Advanced Adventure Diver course is for you! The SDI Advanced Adventure course will give you an overview of five different SDI specialties. Two of the required specialties are the Deep Diver and Navigation Diver specialties which are the foundation of continuing diver education. The remaining specialty dives we will do are:

Advanced Buoyancy Control
Night Diving
Wreck Diving

You will also have the option of doing your Nitrox certification as an add-on to the course for 150$, please contact Flemming if this is something you are interested in.

The course fee includes 2x boat dives but does not include transport, airfills and gear rental for the 5 dives.

Students must have full scuba gear, including diving computer, SMB, compass and two dive lights.

If suitable club gear is not available, I can help you access special pricing on rentals/purchases of dive gear through local dive stores.

There will be an opt-in option for the group to stay at an Airbnb closer to the dive sites during the course.

Any questions, please feel free to contact Flemming at 
[redacted email] or WhatsApp [redacted phone]

events.description:
Are you ready to take the plunge and explore the incredible world beneath the waves? Join our Open Water Diver Course and unlock the gateway to breathtaking underwater adventures.

What You'll Learn:
✅ Essential diving skills
✅ Safety procedures
✅ Equipment use and maintenance
✅ Experience open water dives

Who Can Join?
This course is perfect for beginners and anyone looking to start their scuba diving journey. No prior experience required – just a passion for adventure!

You must be able to swim before starting the course.

What’s Included:
🌟 Professional instruction
🌟 Course materials
🌟 Certification upon completion

Itinerary:
17th April: Pool Day 5pm-9pm
18th April: Ocean Day 1 ALL DAY
19th April: Ocean Day 2 Signoff Day ALL DAY

Sign Up Today!
Spaces are limited, so don’t miss your chance to explore the underwater world.

To register:
1. Read our training policy at: muuc.org.au/courses/training-policy
2. Create an account on Teamapp including your first and last name 
3. Purchase ticket in this event to secure your spot
4. RSVP yes to the event
5. Make sure to track notifications on this event for official course comms.

Prior to the course:
1. Make sure to go to the teamapp store and do the Liability Waiver, Gear Deposit and purchase the applicable membership category to be eligible to receive the e-learning that must be completed prior to starting the practical component of the course. Please complete ASAP

2. Complete your e-learning after receiving the code from your instructor, the e-learning must be completed at least 1 week prior to the course starting. 

3. Make sure to complete your course orientation as per the request of your instructor

Course inquiries:
Use comments section here or email us:
MUUC Course Coordinator:
[redacted email]
SDI Instructor Minh:
[redacted email]

events.event_name: Special General Meeting
events.title: Special General Meeting
events.description:
Dear Members, 

I hope this message finds you well. 

We would like to propose a proactive AGM committee election change. Currently, we are only able to accept nominations for one position. We would like to increase the nominations to two to allow for more members to participate. On April 23rd, we will hold an SGM at 6 pm. 
Location: Melbourne University Dive Shed
Time: 23 April 2026, 6:00 pm
Proposed Amendments:

 Constitution 23. Election of officers and ordinary committee members
1) Nominations of candidates for the election of officers of the Club or as ordinary members of the committee must be-
(a) made in writing, signed by two members of the Club and
accompanied by the written consent of the candidate (which may be
endorsed on the form of nomination); and
(b) delivered to the Secretary of the Club
2) A candidate may be nominated for up to two offices, or for up to one office if also nominated as an ordinary member of the committee, prior to the annual general meeting.

3) A candidate may only be elected for one office or as a general member of the Committee

4)   If insufficient nominations are received to fill all vacancies on the committee, then further nominations may be received at the annual general meeting.

5) Should no more nominations be available, the candidates nominated shall be deemed to be elected. 

6) If more than one nomination for an office is received, a ballot must be held.

7) The ballot for the election of officers and ordinary members of the committee
must be conducted at the annual general meeting in such a manner as the committee may direct.

If you have any questions or concerns, please don’t hesitate to email or call me. I look forward to discussing these points at the SGM.

Burak Uslu
[redacted email]

events.event_name: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
events.title: Computer Nitrox Course with Instructor Minh
events.description:
The course to unlock the best of Victorian Boat Diving! Our boat dives are deep and bottom times are often all too short! With Nitrox diving you can tailor your gas mix to extend your bottom times, and enhance your safety profiles. As the club is equipped with a nitrox compressor, it's never been easier to utilise a nitrox certification!

SDI Computer Nitrox
Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

To attend this course, please purchase a ticketand contact Minh via email to organise for your e-learning to be sent out and plan a suitable date for your practical signoff.
email: [redacted email]

events.event_name: SDI Open Water Course (Instructor Minh) May 1st-3rd
events.title: SDI Open Water Course (Instructor Minh) May 1st-3rd
events.description:
Are you ready to take the plunge and explore the incredible world beneath the waves? Join our Open Water Diver Course and unlock the gateway to breathtaking underwater adventures.

What You'll Learn:
✅ Essential diving skills
✅ Safety procedures
✅ Equipment use and maintenance
✅ Experience open water dives

Who Can Join?
This course is perfect for beginners and anyone looking to start their scuba diving journey. No prior experience required – just a passion for adventure!

You must be able to swim before starting the course.

What’s Included:
🌟 Professional instruction
🌟 Course materials
🌟 Certification upon completion

Itinerary:
1st May: Pool Day 5pm-9pm
2nd May: Ocean Day 1 ALL DAY
3rd May: Ocean Day 2 Signoff Day ALL DAY

Sign Up Today!
Spaces are limited, so don’t miss your chance to explore the underwater world.

To register:
1. Read our training policy at: muuc.org.au/courses/training-policy
2. Create an account on Teamapp including your first and last name 
3. Purchase ticket in this event to secure your spot
4. RSVP yes to the event
5. Make sure to track notifications on this event for official course comms.

Prior to the course:
1. Make sure to go to the teamapp store and do the Liability Waiver, Gear Deposit and purchase the applicable membership category to be eligible to receive the e-learning that must be completed prior to starting the practical component of the course. Please complete ASAP

2. Complete your e-learning after receiving the code from your instructor, the e-learning must be completed at least 1 week prior to the course starting. 

3. Make sure to complete your course orientation as per the request of your instructor

Course inquiries:
Use comments section here or email us:
MUUC Course Coordinator:
[redacted email]
SDI Instructor Minh:
[redacted email]

events.event_name: MUUC watches Shark Kingdom 3D in IMAX!
events.title: MUUC watches Shark Kingdom 3D in IMAX!
events.description:
Join your fellow MUUC members for a screening of the incredible Shark Kingdom 3D!

This is a fantastic opportunity to experience the underwater world like never before. We encourage all members to take advantage of this unique cinematic adventure revealing how the ocean's top predator uses hidden super senses to be a key player in the marine ecosystem.

Each shark species wields unique powers to help them survive and protect their diverse habitats, whether it’s a great white settling in the wild and wind-swept seas of New Zealand, whaler sharks learning to coexist with Australian fishermen, or black tip reef sharks venturing from Tahiti mangroves to the open ocean…

In some of the world’s best diving locations like The Bahamas and Cook Islands, get up close to these awe-inspiring creatures and be immersed in the larger story of our shared oceans.

To secure your spot, please book your ticket via the following link: https://imaxmelbourne.com.au/movie_pricing/Shark-Kingdom-3D

We look forward to seeing you there for this exciting event!

events.event_name: Expressions of Interest - SDI Divemaster
events.title: Expressions of Interest - SDI Divemaster
events.description:
I'm looking at running a Divemaster programme, integrating the learning and course requirements into club trips and club courses.

My vision is for the course to run similar to a Divemaster internship, but with the focus being on MUUC Club activities. 

If you've considered becoming an SDI Divemaster, and would like the opportunity to complete the course far below the normal price, now might be the time. 

This is especially relevant to club trip organisers, boat drivers, those wanting to become more active and help run club diving courses, or those divers just looking to increase their skills in diving and dive safety. 

For those interested, I will look at organising an info-evening to coincide with a club meeting in May.

This course will be run for club members at a fee that covers the cost-price of materials, fees and some costs incurred. 

Check out the prerequisites and course requirements in the SDI Leadership standards attached.

events.description:
**Always First Wednesday of the Month 6pm**

——
**Trip Info**
——
Dive into excitement at our Monthly Scuba Pool Training Session! Whether you’re mastering the basics or fine-tuning advanced techniques, this immersive experience is perfect for all skill levels. Practice buoyancy control, refine navigation, and build confidence in a controlled, safe environment. With expert trainers and a focus on fun, it’s the perfect way to keep your scuba skills sharp and your underwater spirit alive. Don’t just train—transform your dive game!

Please let us know any specific skill requests ahead of the session. 

Session fee: $30, gear included. 

——
**Trip Details**
——
**Trip Leader**: Maciej 
**Secondary Trip Contact**: Amanda
**No. of Planned Dives**:  1.5h
**Mandatory Equipment**: FUN
**Water Temperature**: 26C
**Weather Conditions**: Pool
**Please note that the event start time is the meeting time at the shed unless stated otherwise in the briefing**

——
**Dive Sites / Itinerary**

events.event_name: David Attenborough Watch Party!
events.title: David Attenborough Watch Party!
events.description:
The Melbourne University Underwater Club warmly invites you to a special screening event in celebration of the 100th birthday of David Attenborough.

For many of us, Attenborough’s documentaries were our first introduction to the underwater world — from vibrant coral reefs to the deep sea’s most extraordinary creatures. His work has inspired generations of divers and ocean enthusiasts, and continues to shape how we understand and appreciate marine environments today.

Join us for a relaxed evening watching a selection of his documentaries, with popcorn, good company, and plenty of ocean appreciation.

This event will be at Social Space A!

*Conditions look good for a Sunday morning departure through the river. The plan is to do the first dive on HMAS Canberra, 2nd dive on J4 or Canberra.*

Please bring a bottle of water or a thermos of tea/coffee, lunch/snacks and sun protection. 
Please have breakfast beforehand, or bring something to eat in the car on the way to the dive site.

*It is important that we leave the shed on time*, so please arrive at the shed in time to assemble your gear and be ready for departure. There will be an opportunity to prep gear the day before the dive as well - I’ll be at the shed from 16:30.

- 0800 ramp
- 0900 water

events.event_name: Annual General Meeting
events.title: Annual General Meeting

description_sections.title: Details
body: Welcome to mid-2026. We are having a spectacular year already. We are a very active club in Melbourne University Sports, join us to explore what Melbourne underwater world has to offer.

It is time to assemble and elect the Committee for this year. This is where you – our community, vote for the new members of the committee, as well as nominate and can be nominated for open positions. This is also a great occasion to socialise and discuss any pressing matters for the club. In the finale, the current committee will give you a rundown of our progress so far!

We have been one of the most active teams and we had more trips than any other club under Melbourne University Sports. Please join us to give us a fresh start to both the new year and the school year and we are sure we will have an amazing year of diving.
It is the ordinary business of the AGM to nominate new committee members:
Nominations must be delivered to the Secretary (Burak) no less than 7 days before the date fixed for the holding of the annual general meeting;
Made in writing, signed by two members of the Club and accompanied by the candidate's written consent (which may be endorsed on the form of nomination).
A candidate may only be nominated for up to two office, or for up to one office if also nominated as an ordinary member of the committee, prior to the annual general meeting
A candidate may only be elected for one office or as a general member of the Committee

If insufficient nominations are received to fill all vacancies on the committee, the candidates nominated shall be deemed to be elected and further nominations may be received at the annual general meeting.
If the number of nominations received equals the number of vacancies to be filled, the persons nominated shall be deemed to be elected.
If the number of nominations exceeds the number of vacancies to be filled, a ballot must be held.
( a) to confirm the minutes of the previous annual and special general meeting and of any general meeting held since that meeting; and
( b) to receive from the committee reports upon the transactions of the Club during the last preceding financial year; and
( c) to pass new regulations to our club rules and regulations
( d) to elect officers of the Club and the ordinary members of the committee;

( a) President: The President represents the club inside Melbourne University and outside. Coordinates committee meetings, club efforts and external communications. President oversees all club activities and liaises with Melbourne University Sports ;
( b) Vice-President/Safety Officer: fills in for the President in their absence and assures a safe club environment;
( c) Treasurer: Club's accountant and ensuring the club stays sustainable. Provides monthly cash flow updates and reports this to Melbourne University and Victorian Public Affairs.;
( d) Secretary/Public Officer: keep all paperwork up to date, all external communications and paperwork are intact with Victorian Public Affairs and Melbourne University ;
( e) Gear Officer: Maintain and look after all scuba equipment, and schedules and ensure all gears, tanks, and compressors are regularly maintained.;
( f) Dive Coordinator: help coordinate weekly diving trips, help divers with an extent of local diving knowledge;
( g) Course Coordinator: Work with instructors and students and organise scuba courses.;
( h) Social Officer: Organise social events: BBQ, and movie nights, as well as help with social media communications;
( i) Boat Officer: Look after and maintain our lovely Blue Devil;
( j) Ordinary Member (club car): help with all club activities, and trips as well as maintaining our car; and
( k) Ordinary Member (webmaster): help with all club activities, and trips as well as help with social media communications.
Here is an example nomination email to [redacted email]:
I want to nominate Jane Doe for the Ordinary Member of the committee in the coming AGM. I talked to Jane, and she accepted the nomination. John Smith seconded Jane as a nominee.
c) Two names that nominated Jane: Joe Blows and John Smith.
d) Jane Doe, Joe Blows, and John Smith should all be current 2026 members; that's all.
e) I only need a receipt, one email or a WhatsApp message by May 14, 7 pm.

events.event_name: Gooddivers/ Advanced Nitrox Decompression Divers Course
events.title: Gooddivers/ Advanced Nitrox Decompression Divers Course
events.description:
Seeing the interest for Candidates for ANDP course later this year. 
Will follow up with guidance for preparations. whilst awaiting for the course.
reference the webpage of www.gooddivers.com.au 
MUUC member will get 10% off from the list of courses.

Elevate your technical diving skills with Gooddivers. Advanced Nitrox Decompression Divers course. This intensive program is minimum of certified Advanced Open Water Divers (or equivalent) with at least 100 logged dives, including 25 in open water deeper than 18 meters/60 feet. You must also be a certified Nitrox diver.- Would need some time for level-up with minimum prerequisite.

This course will equip you with the knowledge and techniques to plan and execute dives utilising enriched air nitrox (EANX) up to 100% for decompression. You'll learn advanced decompression theory, gas management, emergency procedures, and the use of specialised equipment for complex dives.

Prepare for challenging dives and expand your underwater exploration capabilities. Upon successful completion, you will be certified to perform decompression dives using decompression stage tanks.

events.title: Jervis Bay Expedition [Boat][AOW+][Multi-Day]
events.description:
——
*Trip Info*
——
An epic trip to Jervis bay and the beautiful Sapphire Coast, staying in an airbnb in Jervis Bay followed by being hosted by Burak at his idyllic Tura Beach house. Driving up to Jervis Bay on the 27th June, diving till the 3rd July, then driving down to Tura Beach/Merimbula. Diving Merimbula/Greencape from the 3rd July- 5th July with Burak. Travelling home on the 6th July.
June 27th - July 6th (7 spots)
1 spot available for June 27th-July 4th (seeking interested parties) this shorter spot will travel back to Melbourne on the 4th via public transport (unless they want to hang out and enjoy the sapphire coast by land).
Deposit of $300 to pay for accomodation
Expected costs of approx. $1000 for all diving, fuel and accommodation. Food will largely be home cooked meals to keep costs down.
To express interest in this trip, please message the trip organiser Kit Hebbard
——
*Trip Details*
——

*Secondary Trip Contact*: JP Ogier
*No. of Planned Dives*:  16

*Weather Conditions*: We will likely have a mixed bag of weather given the length of the trip, but fingers crossed we will have good weather windows.
*Please note that the event start time is the meeting time at the shed unless stated otherwise in the briefing*

Jervis Bay

——
1. Signed off on all cabinet gear taken

events.event_name: Gooddivers -CDAA/TDI Basic cave course
events.title: Gooddivers -CDAA/TDI Basic cave course
events.description:
Into the Dark – CDAA/TDI Basic Cave Course

Ready to swap the ocean for the magical sinkholes of Mount Gambier? We are teaming up with Gooddivers to run the CDAA/TDI Basic Cave Course this July! 
This is your entry-level ticket to the world of overhead environments, focusing on perfect buoyancy, reel work, and team awareness.

🗓️ When: Mid July
📍 Location: option 1. Theory & Pool in Melbourne | Dives in Mount Gambier, SA.
                             option 2. Mount Gambier for 5 days.
💰 Cost: A$1075  (includes materials and CDAA membership).

## What You’ll Learn:

* Technical Precision: Master your trim, buoyancy, and anti-silting finning techniques.
* Line Work: Learn to lay, follow, and manage primary reels and markers (cookies).
* Emergency Drills: S-drills, valve shutdowns, and zero-visibility navigation.
* Exploration: Dive world-class sites like Kilsby’s Sinkhole - subject availability

## Prerequisites:
Before we head underground, you’ll need:

* Certification: PADI Advanced Open Water (or equivalent) for at least 12 months.
* Experience: Minimum 50 logged dives (25+ hours), including 2 night dives and 5 dives deeper than 20m.
* Age: 18+ years.
* Medical: A current diving medical statement.

## Gear Requirements:
Cave diving requires a specific setup. If you don't have your own, www.gooddivers.com.au/about-us can assist with rentals:

* Twin cylinders (manifolded or independent) or Sidemount configuration.
* Two regulator first stages with a 2-metre long hose.
* Primary light + two backup lights.
* Exposure suit suitable for ~15°C (Drysuit highly recommended).
* Primary reel (min 100m will be provided) and personal line markers.

## How to Join:

   1. Express Interest: Hit "YES" or "Interested" to stay updated on the July date announcement.
   2. Contact : Reach out via Gooddivers Contact or email [redacted email].
   3. MUUC Meeting: Come chat with us at the Shed on Thursday at 7 PM!.

Let’s go find some clear water!

With Nitrox diving you can tailor your gas mix to extend your bottom times, and enhance your safety profiles. As the club is equipped with a nitrox compressor, it's never been easier to utilise a nitrox certification!

SDI Computer Nitrox
Dive longer and enhance safety with Nitrox certification, the choice gas for discerning divers. The SDI Computer Nitrox course equips you with the know-how to safely harness Nitrox’s benefits, enriching your dives with gas mixtures from 22% to 40% oxygen.

To attend this course, please purchase a ticketand contact Minh via email to organise for your e-learning to be sent out and plan a suitable date for your practical signoff.
email: [redacted email]

events.event_name: [POOL]  September Pool Training
events.title: [POOL]  September Pool Training
events.description:
**Always First Wednesday of the Month 6pm**

——
**Trip Info**
——
Dive into excitement at our Monthly Scuba Pool Training Session! Whether you’re mastering the basics or fine-tuning advanced techniques, this immersive experience is perfect for all skill levels. Practice buoyancy control, refine navigation, and build confidence in a controlled, safe environment. With expert trainers and a focus on fun, it’s the perfect way to keep your scuba skills sharp and your underwater spirit alive. Don’t just train—transform your dive game!

Please let us know any specific skill requests ahead of the session. 

Session fee: $30, gear included. 

——
**Trip Details**
——
**Trip Leader**: Maciej 
**Secondary Trip Contact**: Amanda
**No. of Planned Dives**:  1.5h
**Mandatory Equipment**: FUN
**Water Temperature**: 26C
**Weather Conditions**: Pool
**Please note that the event start time is the meeting time at the shed unless stated otherwise in the briefing**

——
**Dive Sites / Itinerary**
——
6pm meet at the shed to prepare
6:50pm shed closes 
7pm-8:45pm pool session

——
**PARTICIPATION REQUIREMENTS**
——
1. Current Membership 
2. Current Liability Waiver 
3. Current Certificate suitable for trip level.  
4. Boat Trips: 
    - Cleared by Dive Coordinator 
    - Cleared by this trip Boat Master 
    - Demonstrates sufficient skill level 

——
**Gear Hire Policy**
——
1. Signed off on all cabinet gear taken 
2. Has current gear hire deposit

hero_spotlight_events.title: Jervis Bay Expedition [Boat][AOW+][Multi-Day]
hero_spotlight_events.description:
——
*Trip Info*
——
An epic trip to Jervis bay and the beautiful Sapphire Coast, staying in an airbnb in Jervis Bay followed by being hosted by Burak at his idyllic Tura Beach house. Driving up to Jervis Bay on the 27th June, diving till the 3rd July, then driving down to Tura Beach/Merimbula. Diving Merimbula/Greencape from the 3rd July- 5th July with Burak. Travelling home on the 6th July.
June 27th - July 6th (7 spots)
1 spot available for June 27th-July 4th (seeking interested parties) this shorter spot will travel back to Melbourne on the 4th via public transport (unless they want to hang out and enjoy the sapphire coast by land).
Deposit of $300 to pay for accomodation
Expected costs of approx. $1000 for all diving, fuel and accommodation. Food will largely be home cooked meals to keep costs down.
To express interest in this trip, please message the trip organiser Kit Hebbard
——
*Trip Details*
——
*Trip Leader*:  Kit Hebbard
*Secondary Trip Contact*: JP Ogier
*No. of Planned Dives*:  16
*Mandatory Equipment*: E.g. torches, SMB, Tent, Sleeping Bag, Sleeping Mat (please message Kit if you don't have camping equipment as arrangements can be made)
*Water Temperature*: 19°C
*Weather Conditions*: We will likely have a mixed bag of weather given the length of the trip, but fingers crossed we will have good weather windows.
*Please note that the event start time is the meeting time at the shed unless stated otherwise in the briefing*

——
*Dive Sites / Itinerary*
——
Jervis Bay
Merimbula
Tathra
Eden
Greencape

——
*PARTICIPATION REQUIREMENTS*
——
1. Current Membership 
2. Current Liability Waiver 
3. Current Certificate suitable for trip level.  
4. Boat Trips: 
    - Cleared by Dive Coordinator 
    - Cleared by this trip Boat Master 
    - Demonstrates sufficient skill level 
    - Please ensure for boat trips that the ticket/store boat item has been bought 24 hours prior                 to the event or your spot will be forfeited to the next person.
5. Buy gear hire prior to trip occurring. 

——
*Gear Hire Policy*
——
1. Signed off on all cabinet gear taken 
https://forms.gle/WjdJsYN9Qrv1iahY9
2. Has current gear hire deposit

hero_spotlight_events.title: Gooddivers -CDAA/TDI Basic cave course
hero_spotlight_events.description:
Into the Dark – CDAA/TDI Basic Cave Course

Ready to swap the ocean for the magical sinkholes of Mount Gambier? We are teaming up with Gooddivers to run the CDAA/TDI Basic Cave Course this July! 
This is your entry-level ticket to the world of overhead environments, focusing on perfect buoyancy, reel work, and team awareness.

🗓️ When: Mid July
📍 Location: option 1. Theory & Pool in Melbourne | Dives in Mount Gambier, SA.
                             option 2. Mount Gambier for 5 days.
💰 Cost: A$1075  (includes materials and CDAA membership).

## What You’ll Learn:

* Technical Precision: Master your trim, buoyancy, and anti-silting finning techniques.
* Line Work: Learn to lay, follow, and manage primary reels and markers (cookies).
* Emergency Drills: S-drills, valve shutdowns, and zero-visibility navigation.
* Exploration: Dive world-class sites like Kilsby’s Sinkhole - subject availability

## Prerequisites:
Before we head underground, you’ll need:

* Certification: PADI Advanced Open Water (or equivalent) for at least 12 months.
* Experience: Minimum 50 logged dives (25+ hours), including 2 night dives and 5 dives deeper than 20m.
* Age: 18+ years.
* Medical: A current diving medical statement.

## Gear Requirements:
Cave diving requires a specific setup. If you don't have your own, www.gooddivers.com.au/about-us can assist with rentals:

* Twin cylinders (manifolded or independent) or Sidemount configuration.
* Two regulator first stages with a 2-metre long hose.
* Primary light + two backup lights.
* Exposure suit suitable for ~15°C (Drysuit highly recommended).
* Primary reel (min 100m will be provided) and personal line markers.

## How to Join:

   1. Express Interest: Hit "YES" or "Interested" to stay updated on the July date announcement.
   2. Contact : Reach out via Gooddivers Contact or email [redacted email].
   3. MUUC Meeting: Come chat with us at the Shed on Thursday at 7 PM!.

Let’s go find some clear water!

* Primary reel (min 100m will be provided) and personal line markers.
1. Express Interest: Hit "YES" or "Interested" to stay updated on the July date announcement.
2. Contact : Reach out via Gooddivers Contact or email [redacted email].
3. MUUC Meeting: Come chat with us at the Shed on Thursday at 7 PM!.
Let’s go find some clear water!
